disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden-onset severe upper-mid abdominal pain that bores through to the back, constant for ~4 hours
trigger_question_examples: Where is the pain and does it go anywhere?
How did the pain start and how bad is it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's high in the middle of my belly, really severe, and it bores straight through to my back. It came on suddenly about four hours ago and hasn't let up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain partially relieved by sitting up and leaning forward, worse lying flat
trigger_question_examples: Is there any position that makes it better or worse?
Does anything ease the pain at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It eases just a little if I sit up and lean forward. Lying flat makes it worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profuse persistent vomiting with complete inability to tolerate oral fluids — dehydration risk
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to keep any fluids down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I keep vomiting and I can't keep even a sip of water down. Nothing stays in.

trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions?
How much alcohol do you drink?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been told I have gallstones. I don't really drink alcohol.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels sweaty, shaky and clammy with the pain
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel sweaty, feverish or shaky?
How are you feeling in yourself apart from the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel really sweaty and shaky, and clammy all over.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No blood in the vomit and no black/tarry stools reported (pertinent negative screened on questioning)
trigger_question_examples: Have you seen any blood in the vomit, or has it looked like coffee grounds?
Have your stools been black or tarry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't seen any blood when I'm sick, and my stools have been normal.
